problem_text: Use a comparison test to determine whether sum_{n=1}^infinity 1/(2n + 1) converges or diverges.

content.label: Positive-term series
content.applies_when: the terms are positive, so a comparison test applies

content.meaning: the harmonic series sum 1/n is the p-series with p = 1 and diverges.
if lim a_n/b_n = c is finite and positive, then sum a_n and sum b_n converge or diverge together.

content.label: compare with the harmonic series b_n = 1/n
content.action: compare with the harmonic series b_n = 1/n
content.purpose: select a comparison series matching the 1/n growth

content.label: compute lim [1/(2n+1)]/[1/n] = lim n/(2n + 1) = 1/2, a finite positive number
content.action: compute lim [1/(2n+1)]/[1/n] = lim n/(2n + 1) = 1/2, a finite positive number
content.purpose: form the limit ratio required by the Limit Comparison Test

content.label: Finite positive limit
content.applies_when: the limit c = 1/2 is finite and positive
content.transformation: sum a_n and sum b_n share the same convergence behavior

content.label: since the harmonic series sum 1/n diverges and c = 1/2 > 0, sum 1/(2n + 1) diverges
content.action: since the harmonic series sum 1/n diverges and c = 1/2 > 0, sum 1/(2n + 1) diverges
